---
title: "Laboratory of Wildlife Ecology and Behaviour"
---

![](Images/0.jpg){fig-align="center"}

::: {style="text-align: justify;"}
Welcome to the Laboratory of Wildlife Ecology & Behaviour at **University College Dublin, Ireland**.
:::

![](Images/exploded%20lab%20logo.jpg){fig-align="center"}

::: {style="text-align: justify;"}
We observe, monitor, and track wildlife to learn more about its ecology and behaviour, and improve its management and conservation within human-dominated landscapes. We work towards a better coexistence between wildlife and humans.
:::

```{=html}
<style>
.collage {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 260px);
    gap: 10px;
    justify-content: center;
}
.collage div {
    width: 260px;
}
.collage img {
    width: 100%;
    height: auto;
    display: block;
}
</style>
```

::::::::: collage
<div>

<img src="Images/1.JPG" alt="Image 1"/>

</div>

<div>

<img src="Images/2.JPG" alt="Image 2"/>

</div>

<div>

<img src="Images/3.jpg" alt="Image 3"/>

</div>

<div>

<img src="Images/4.JPG" alt="Image 4"/>

</div>

<div>

<img src="Images/5.JPG" alt="Image 5"/>

</div>

<div>

<img src="Images/6.JPG" alt="Image 6"/>
```{r}

```

</div>
:::::::::

::: {style="text-align: justify;"}
Our research expertise spans animal ecology, movement and behavioural ecology, field-based research, satellite radiotracking and biologging, and advanced statistical modelling. We work closely with policymakers and government agencies to translate our research into evidence-based management and conservation solutions, with tangible impacts on the future management of wildlife populations at national and international scales.
:::

![](Images/sanrossore.JPG){fig-align="center" width="932"} ![](Images/Namibia.jpg){fig-align="center"}
